source:
# Run in a Synapse Spark notebook

# 1 — Check how the table is defined in the metastore
spark.sql("DESCRIBE EXTENDED `Database1`.`voters_postgresql`").show(100, truncate=False)

# Drop existing
spark.sql("DROP TABLE IF EXISTS `Database1`.`voters_postgresql`")

# Recreate pointing at correct path
# Synapse will infer schema from the Parquet files
spark.sql("""
    CREATE TABLE `Database1`.`voters_postgresql`
    USING PARQUET
    LOCATION 'abfss://juror-etl@baubaisadfsastg.dfs.core.windows.net/voters_postgresql'
""")

# Repair partitions if data is partitioned
spark.sql("MSCK REPAIR TABLE `Database1`.`voters_postgresql`")

# Verify
count = spark.sql("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM `Database1`.`voters_postgresql`").collect()[0][0]
print(f"✅ Row count: {count}")

# Drop if already exists
spark.sql("DROP TABLE IF EXISTS `Database1`.`voters_deduplicated_delta`")

# Create as Delta pointing to your container
spark.sql("""
    CREATE TABLE `Database1`.`voters_deduplicated_delta`
    USING DELTA
    LOCATION 'abfss://dl-juror-eric-voters-temp@baubaisadfsastg.dfs.core.windows.net/voters_deduplicated_delta'
""")

# Verify
count = spark.sql("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM `Database1`.`voters_deduplicated_delta`").collect()[0][0]
print(f"✅ Row count: {count}")

# Show sample
spark.sql("SELECT * FROM `Database1`.`voters_deduplicated_delta` LIMIT 5").show(truncate=False)

# Force sync Spark catalog with Lake Database metastore
spark.sql("REFRESH TABLE `Database1`.`voters_deduplicated_delta`")

# Confirm it's registered
spark.sql("SHOW TABLES IN `Database1`").show(truncate=False)
